findings.title: Manual refresh resets the countdown display but not the actual auto-refresh timer
findings.evidence: startAutoRefresh creates one 60000ms setInterval (L635-639); btnRefresh (L901) only calls loadApi(); loadApi finally updates refreshState.nextRefreshAt (L877) but there is no clearInterval/timer reschedule anywhere in the file (grep confirmed: no clearInterval/clearTimeout). A manual refresh at T+50s resets the countdown target to T+110s while the original interval still fires at T+60s.
findings.impact: The UI can show 'next refresh' with ~50s remaining while an automatic refresh fires immediately. This violates user decision C ('manual refresh resets the 60s countdown'): the reset is display-only, not aligned with the actual next auto-fetch.
findings.recommendation: Reschedule the auto-refresh timer whenever loadApi completes (incl. manual refresh + failure), so the displayed nextRefreshAt and the actual next fetch share the same 60s target.

required_fixes: In frontend/index.html, make the actual auto-refresh timer use the same nextRefreshAt target as the countdown. Minimal fix: in loadApi's finally block, after setting nextRefreshAt, clearInterval(refreshState.refreshTimer) and recreate the 60000ms timer; keep the 1s countdown interval separate.

Preserve same-origin fetch('/api/public-market/snapshot'); do not reintroduce btn-offline/loadFixture/'fixture' branch; do NOT modify formatFundingRate/formatBeijing/formatBeijingShort; no backend/schema/docs-contract/api-samples changes.
Extend frontend/self-check.js to cover the manual-refresh timer reset (mock setInterval/clearInterval; assert a manual refresh invalidates the old 60s timer and schedules a new one).
Rerun: node frontend/self-check.js; PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider backend/tests -q; offline snapshot jsonschema validation.
residual_risks: lastFundingRate evidence is a single mid-period snapshot with drift; accepted residual, not a rework blocker.
